notes: v1.14.38 - globe un-mirror + city-light densification, both JS-only in the landing globe IIFE (no markup/CSS). (1) UN-MIRROR: negate uScale.x in BOTH render calls (gloc textured sphere + loc point cloud) so the entire rendered globe (terrain, city dots, arcs, packets, storm) mirrors together about screen-center, fixing the east-west flip - the US was reversed and London sat on the wrong limb. Both programs project identically (gl_Position.x = uCenter.x + x*uScale.x), so the two negations stay perfectly registered; backface discard (vN.z / z2) and fresnel (N.z) are normal-based and untouched; no CULL_FACE and DEPTH_TEST is disabled, so winding is irrelevant; Austin stays dead-center. (2) CITY DETAIL: supersample the SPECTRUM seeding loop - each bright cell now emits up to 6 dots (ns = 1 + floor(lf*lf*5)) instead of one per half-degree cell, so metro cores build a dense glowing core while rural stays a single speckle; per-dot brightness kept modest (nbr = 0.55 + lf*0.80 +/- jitter) to avoid OLED white-out. City dots ~9,800 to ~18,900 (1.94x), GPU buffer 0.30 to 0.58 MB, still one draw call. ENTER_ENABLED stays false - John is still inspecting, no auto-warp this ship. GATES: node --check 3/3 inline + sw.js PASS; four stamps lockstep v1.14.38; cache bumped; line endings preserved (dct/sw CRLF, this file LF); Block-0 CSS untouched (brace baseline 2760, no re-count). DEVICE-CHECK (John, iPhone): geography should now read correct (US not mirrored, London on the RIGHT limb); metro cores denser and brighter; if any additive pile-to-white on OLED at DPR3, dial the 5 in lf*lf*5 down (do NOT raise brightness); auto-spin and drag will appear reversed (cosmetic - flip rotY += dx*0.008 to -= if it bugs).
